title: podman remembers --arch across invocations
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Podman remembers the last --arch I used in a `podman run` invocation and uses it again later (if I use the same image name).
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create this script

```
#!/bin/bash -e

podman rmi docker.io/fedora:33
podman run --rm --arch s390x docker.io/fedora:33 uname -a
podman run --rm docker.io/fedora:33 uname -a
```

2. Run the script

3. Observe that the output of the two uname commands is the same. This would be expected only if you run it on an s390x machine.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Linux 4a0705059b64 5.12.0-0.rc0.20210222git31caf8b2a847.158.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Feb 22 15:59:16 UTC 2021 s390x s390x s390x GNU/Linux
Linux 82045ab1e86d 5.12.0-0.rc0.20210222git31caf8b2a847.158.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Feb 22 15:59:16 UTC 2021 s390x s390x s390x GNU/Linux
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
Linux 4a0705059b64 5.12.0-0.rc0.20210222git31caf8b2a847.158.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Feb 22 15:59:16 UTC 2021 s390x s390x s390x GNU/Linux
Linux 82045ab1e86d 5.12.0-0.rc0.20210222git31caf8b2a847.158.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Feb 22 15:59:16 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I expect the storage needs to refer to image manifests as first-class citizens and resolve `docker.io/fedora:33` as an image manifest, not the last image that happened to be used under that name.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 18:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  cpus: 12

  kernel: 5.12.0-0.rc0.20210222git31caf8b2a847.158.fc35.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 3220312064

  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.18-1.fc33.x86_64

      crun version 0.18

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux

  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.0.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

It seems that the local-image lookup will match `--arch=""` with a non-native architecture.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vrothberg Is this something libimage will fix?

I thought @mtrmac did not want us to automatically set the arch and os flags when we call into containers/image, but allow containers/image to figure it out.  But in this case we are looking at local storage and noticing the difference.
Yes, we can account for that in the new lib.

This doesn’t look like something where setting, or not, an architecture option into c/image should make any difference.

The storage is not split by architecture, and `containers-storage:` names in c/image are not architecture-bound. (The `c/storage.Image` might not be even annotated with architecture, per the earlier auto-update issue.) So, once a `docker.io/fedora:33` exist locally, lookups for an image `docker.io/fedora:33` will succeed locally, regardless of any architecture options. Changing `SystemContext` should not make any difference.

---

Actually there is `c/image/storage.imageMatchesSystemContext`, I’m not sure this does the right thing (it checks based on an artificial manifest instead of the right one with all the relevant variant alternatives), but either way it only seems to be used for digested references.

I just went back to the issue and think the behavior is sane.

Without `--arch` Podman attempts to use the image as specified by the user.  It may very well work with a non-native image if the underlying runtime is using an emulator.

That being said, if there's a local image `foo` and we do a `run foo` it will be used as is.  A `run --arch=amd64` however would realize that the local image does not match the requested architecture and pull down the matching image.

@rhatdan, WDYT?

That is what I would want correct.

```
$ uname -a
Linux localhost.localdomain 5.13.0-0.rc1.20210511git1140ab592e2e.14.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue May 11 15:09:06 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
$ podman run --rm --arch s390x docker.io/fedora uname -a
Trying to pull docker.io/library/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a81753b6480e done  
Copying config 8cfe45c024 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Linux 0453e22d39a7 5.13.0-0.rc1.20210511git1140ab592e2e.14.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue May 11 15:09:06 UTC 2021 s390x s390x s390x GNU/Linux
$ podman run --rm --arch arm64 docker.io/fedora uname -a
Trying to pull docker.io/library/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e834a7f2e69a done  
Copying config 1b03757256 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Linux a1ff30613f81 5.13.0-0.rc1.20210511git1140ab592e2e.14.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue May 11 15:09:06 UTC 2021 aarch64 aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

$ podman -v
podman version 3.2.0-rc2

Seems to work in master.
I get `Error: docker.io/fedora:33: image not known` in the reproducer. 3.2.0-rc2.
